articles.title: Top Gainers & Losers on 10 July: Godrej Industries, Indian Bank, Kalyan Jewellers, Paytm, CDSL among top gainers
articles.excerpt:
Amid strong support from financial stocks and IT major TCS, the domestic equity markets ended Friday's session, 10July, with solid gains, recovering much of their recent losses. Sentiment was further boosted by a decline in crude oil prices and a recovery in the Indian rupee.

Both the Nifty 50 and Sensex ended the session with gains of over 1%. However, they still closed the week with marginal losses. The broader markets outperformed the benchmark indices, with the Nifty Midcap 100 rising 1.4% and the Nifty Smallcap 100 advancing 1.55%.

Amid broad-based buying, all major sectoral indices—except Nifty FMCG—ended in the green. Nifty Realty once again emerged as the top performer, surging 3.5

articles.title: Kalyan Jewellers India share price jumps over 36% in three days. Buy, sell or hold?
articles.excerpt:
Kalyan Jewellers share price jumped over 8% on Friday, extending its rally for the third consecutive session, led by strong buying momentum. Kalyan Jewellers shares rallied as much as 8.67% to ₹483.10 apiece on the BSE.

In three sessions, Kalyan Jewellers India stock price has jumped more than 36%, lifting its one month gains to over 41%.

The rally in Kalyan Jewellers share price today was supported by robust volumes, as around 8 crore equity shares changed hands on the stock exchanges on July 10, significantly higher than one week average trading volumes of 4 crore shares and one month average volumes of 2 crore shares.

The recent gains in Kalyan Jewellers India shares came after the com

articles.title: Kalyan Jewellers jumps 9%, m-cap swells by Rs 13,280 crore in 3 days. What's next?
articles.excerpt:
Kalyan Jewellers shares saw significant gains after a strong first-quarter business update. The company reported nearly 38% consolidated revenue growth compared to the previous year. This performance occurred despite a challenging period for gold purchases. Analysts maintain a bullish stance, citing expansion strategies and industry formalisation.

articles.title: India’s soybean acreage seen rising 5-7 per cent on favourable prices
articles.excerpt:
As soybean prices rule above the minimum support price levels, farmers in the key producing regions of Central India are showing preference to plant the oilseed crop and the trade body Soybean Processors Association of India (SOPA) expects the acreage to rise by 5-7 per cent over last year’s levels.

Sowing of soybean has almost been complete for the kharif 2026 season in the key growing regions and section of farmers, who had shifted to maize last year, are seen switching back to the oilseed crop due to higher prices.

“Based on our field assessment, soybean sowing has been completed in approximately 80-90 per cent of the targetted area across soybean growing States. We expect the acreage t
articles.citation: India’s soybean acreage seen rising 5-7 per cent on favourable prices — BusinessLine Mkts, 2026-07-09. https://www.thehindubusinessline.com/economy/agri-business/indias-soybean-acreage-seen-rising-5-7-per-cent-on-favourable-prices/article71203124.ece

articles.title: Meta stock surges 7.5% as report says AI chip production could begin this year
articles.excerpt:
Shares of Meta Platforms surged nearly 7.5% in Friday's trade (10 July), climbing to $677 on the Nasdaq and extending its winning streak to a second straight session after a Reuters report said the company is planning to begin manufacturing its own artificial intelligence (AI) chips.

According to the Reuters report, Meta plans to start manufacturing its custom AI chip, code-named “Iris”, with partners Broadcom and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) later this year.

Reuters reported, citing an internal company memo it reviewed, that Meta plans to begin manufacturing the AI chip from September as part of its strategy to expand its overall computing capacity to 14 gigawatts next ye
articles.citation: Meta stock surges 7.5% as report says AI chip production could begin this year — Mint Markets, 2026-07-10. https://www.livemint.com/market/stock-market-news/meta-stock-surges-12-as-report-says-ai-chip-production-could-begin-this-year-11783690729013.html

articles.title: Global Market | Tencent explores stake in AI Startup Manus after China challenges Meta deal: Reports
articles.excerpt:
Tencent is in talks to become the largest shareholder in AI startup Manus as investors seek to buy back the company from Meta after Chinese regulators ordered the US tech giant to unwind its $2 billion acquisition. The proposed deal would reshape Manus' ownership amid heightened regulatory scrutiny of cross-border AI investments.

articles.title: India's 10-year bond logs best day in over a week on oil relief
articles.excerpt:
Indian government bonds rose on Friday, with the benchmark ​yield posting its sharpest fall in more than a week, ‌as softer oil prices, foreign inflows and strong ​demand at a weekly auction lifted ⁠sentiment.

The 6.94% 2036 bond yield fell 3.8 basis points to 6.7139%, sliding the most in a day since July 2. ‌It ended little changed week-on-week. Bond yields move inversely to prices.

Demand at a ₹32 crore ($3.36 ‌billion) sale was strong, including for a 40-year ‌note. ⁠Traders said the cutoffs showed investors were willing ⁠to add duration risk, especially with foreign demand intact.

"Foreign flows and the auction result have given bonds a near-term cushion, but traders ​will stay alert
articles.citation: India's 10-year bond logs best day in over a week on oil relief — BusinessLine Mkts, 2026-07-10. https://www.thehindubusinessline.com/markets/indias-10-year-bond-logs-best-day-in-over-a-week-on-oil-relief/article71206420.ece

articles.title: US Stock Market: Treasury yields ease as investors buy bonds after selloff; strong auction boosts sentiment
articles.excerpt:
US Treasury prices saw gains as investors bought bonds following a sharp selloff. A strong auction of 30-year government bonds attracted significant foreign investor demand. Economic data indicated stable labor and moderating housing markets, reinforcing Fed rate hold expectations. Geopolitical tensions involving the United States and Iran remained a key focus for markets. Investors anticipate stable Federal Reserve interest rates and potential future cuts.

articles.title: More LNG Carriers Brave the Strait of Hormuz Despite Renewed Hostilities
articles.excerpt:
Fuel Refining Margins Hit Record Highs as Markets Tighten

Close to half a dozen liquefied natural gas carriers have gone into the Strait of Hormuz in the past few days and six have exited the strait, despite the recent return to hostilities between the United States and Iran.

According to Kpler data cited by Reuters, five ballast LNG tankers have entered the chokepoint in recent days, four of them linked to Qatar, and the fifth one owned by a Greek shipping company. The five vessels were previously seen outside Hormuz earlier this month.

articles.title: Natural gas companies are set to lose steam in Q1
articles.excerpt:
This is a Mint Premium article gifted to you.Subscribe to enjoy similar stories.

Domestic natural gas companies are bracing for a weak June quarter (Q1FY27) as lower LNG imports and higher gas procurement costs triggered by the West Asia conflict weigh on earnings.

Total domestic natural gas consumption during the first two months of Q1FY27 fell more than 10% year-on-year and 13% compared with February levels to about 170 mmscmd (million standard cubic metres per day), according to the latest data from the Petroleum Planning & Analysis Cell (PPAC).

The decline reflected lower LNG imports and weaker domestic natural gas production, each of them account for nearly half of India's gas consum

articles.title: Morgan Stanley warns AI chip rally may be running out of steam
articles.excerpt:
Morgan Stanley's Lisa Shalett warned semiconductor stocks may be overvalued as hyperscalers increasingly develop lower-cost proprietary AI chips. She expects slowing AI infrastructure spending and weaker pricing power to challenge chipmakers, despite continued investor interest and SK Hynix's Nasdaq debut.

articles.title: Wall Street banks rule on staff betting on prediction markets, sources say
articles.excerpt:
In a significant move, major Wall Street banks have revised their employee conduct guidelines. The updated policies impose restrictions on engaging in financial and political prediction markets. Specifically, Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley have enacted firm prohibitions, while JPMorgan Chase and Bank of America have also established comparable limitations. These initiatives are designed to eliminate conflicts of interest and maintain compliance with industry standards.

articles.title: Wall Street muted, SK Hynix shares rally 14% in Nasdaq debut
articles.excerpt:
US stock markets traded largely flat on Friday as South Korean semiconductor giant SK Hynix rallied in its debut on Nasdaq.

At 11:57 a.m. ET, the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 110.06 points, or 0.21%, to 52,597.47, the S&P 500 gained 9.75 points, or 0.13%, to 7,553.35 and the Nasdaq Composite lost 5.36 points, or 0.02%, to 26,201.53.

At the opening bell, the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 135.5 points, or 0.26%, to 52622.92. The S&P 500 rose 4.0 points, or 0.05%, to 7547.64​, while the Nasdaq Composite dropped 31.4 points, or 0.12%, to 26175.535.

articles.title: Multibagger defence stock Apollo Micro Systems to be in focus on Friday; here's why
articles.excerpt:
Apollo Micro Systems on Thursday announced that it has entered into a definitive Share Purchase Agreement (SPA) with the promoter shareholders of Premier Explosives to acquire a 41.33% stake in the company for approximately ₹1,550 crore in an all-cash deal, subject to customary closing adjustments.

The acquisition forms part of Apollo Micro Systems' long-term strategy to build an integrated, end-to-end indigenous defence platforms ecosystem in line with the Government of India's Aatmanirbhar Bharat and Make in India initiatives.
